channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden's last-minute emissions goal could be quickly reversed when Trump takes office
item.description: President Biden increased the emissions reduction goal for the United States under the Paris climate agreement, which Trump has signaled he intends to withdraw from.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An ex-Washington police officer is convicted of lying about leaks to the Proud Boys leader
item.description: A retired police officer in the nation’s capital has been convicted of lying to authorities about leaking confidential information to the leader of the Proud Boys extremist group.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House Ethics Committee accuses Gaetz of 'regularly' paying for sex, including with 17-year-old girl
item.description: The 37-page report by the bipartisan panel includes explicit details of sex-filled parties and vacations that Matt Gaetz took part in while representing Florida's western panhandle. The findings conclude that he violated multiple state laws related to sexual misconduct while in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez faces uncertain political future
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) is facing an uncertain future after a pivotal setback this week, when she lost her race to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee. The 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly’s (D-Va.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ rep. says it's alarming Feds can't bring down one drone
item.description: Chris Smith is disappointed by what he sees as a lack of transparency by the Biden administration over the New Jersey drone flap.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Democrats condemn ethical lapses by Thomas, Supreme Court justices
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee condemned the Supreme Court for an “ethical crisis of its own making” in a new report released Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Senate review of Supreme Court ethics finds more luxury trips and urges enforceable code of conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A nearly two-year investigation by Democratic senators of Supreme Court ethics details more luxury travel by Justice Clarence Thomas and urges Congress to establish a way to enforce a new code of conduct.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs bill that averts a government shutdown and brings a close to days of Washington upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden signed a bill into law Saturday that averts a government shutdown, bringing a final close to days of upheaval after Congress approved a temporary funding plan just past the deadline and refused President-elect Donald Trump’s core debt demands in the package.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Funding saga forecasts future struggles for Speaker Johnson
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) averted a government shutdown, but the days-long rollercoaster as the leaders cycled through multiple plans leaves serious questions about how the Speaker will manage the House GOP under President-elect Trump — and whether he can keep his gavel.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fani Willis disqualification may doom Trump’s Georgia case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis’s (D) disqualification from Georgia's election interference case against President-elect Trump and his allies could mark the beginning of the end of the prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: A day before a potential government shutdown, the House resoundingly rejected President-elect Donald Trump's new plan to fund operations and suspend the debt ceiling, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown. It now goes to President Joe Biden, who is expected to quickly sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Chip Roy: Trump wants Social Security benefits intact
item.description: Chip Roy believes Trump doesn't actually want to cut from Social Security and other essential benefits on which Americans rely.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Carter takes scalpel to St. Paul budget with line-item veto, approves smaller levy increase
item.description: St. Paul Mayor Melvin Carter used his line-item veto power to cut several items from the city’s 2025 budget, but the city council overrode all of the vetoes at a special meeting late Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dems say 'hell no' to GOP plan B on government funding, debt ceiling
item.description: House Democrats are rallying in overwhelming opposition to the Republicans' second stab at a government funding bill, vowing to sink it on the House floor and force GOP leaders back to the table to negotiate a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates seek to prepare for possible ‘mass deportations’ without fearmongering
item.description: Minnesota immigrant rights groups say they want to avoid evoking fear in their communities, but are finding ways to stay prepared ahead of the next Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: What a potential government shutdown could mean for San Diego
item.description: The federal government is barreling towards a partial shutdown after GOP lawmakers in Congress backed away from a deal on a short-term spending bill struck with Democrats just days before the Friday deadline.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Donald Trump said he wants to ban trans athletes from competing. The reality is more nuanced
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s campaign promise to ‘ban’ transgender athletes struck a chord with voters. It’s uncertain, however, what exactly Trump will do once his second term begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams surrenders to DA's office to face charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams surrendered to authorities Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to the administration of a mayor who has seen who is facing his own indictment and investigations into multiple aides and allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: After losing VP bid, Walz searches for role in pushing back against Trump
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz saw his national profile skyrocket when he became Vice President Harris’ running mate. Now, he’s back home figuring out when he will push back or work with Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Pornhub to block access in Florida amid lawsuit over new age verification law
item.description: Pornhub said it will block access to its website from Florida as an adult entertainment advocacy group sues over the state's new law requiring age verification.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Ethics Committee secretly voted to release Matt Gaetz ethics report, source says
item.description: The House Ethics Committee has voted in secret to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court will hear arguments over the law that could ban TikTok in the U.S. if it's not sold
item.description: The justices said Wednesday they will hear arguments Jan. 10 about whether the law impermissibly restricts speech in violation of the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
